content.claim: Quasi-experimental difference-in-differences regression analysis with matching methods (2010–2018 period) found that Finnish rural community population declined *more* after school closure than before, suggesting closures accelerate rather than merely follow demographic decline.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Analysis period is 8 years; longer-run effects (15+ years) unknown
Causal mechanism (loss of institutional anchor vs. parental out-migration triggered by closure news) not disaggregated; reverse causality partially controlled but not fully eliminated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
